title: Agentic Assurance Profile lite assurance file
description: Schema for `.agentic-assurance/assurance.yaml`, the single assurance file of the lite layout (`layout: lite` in the configured adoption declaration; core profile only). This is a thin envelope: it types the prose fields and names the register sections, but deliberately does not duplicate the register entry shapes. Items in the `invariants`, `defeaters`, and `residuals` sections follow the entry shapes of invariants.schema.json, defeaters.schema.json, and residuals.schema.json respectively; the validator extracts each present section and validates it against the corresponding register schema, so every entry shape is defined in exactly one place. To graduate to an active specialized profile, preserve IDs by moving each present register array unchanged into its split register, move inline system prose into the artifact mapped by `paths.system`, preserve purpose/non_goals there or in another owner-approved local intent artifact, and preserve or deliberately relocate extensions before dropping `layout: lite`. To reclassify as archived, instead write and obtain owner confirmation of all four PROFILE.md section 6.6 facts in the mapped system artifact; retained active registers are optional history and do not substitute for those facts. Unknown top-level keys are rejected (a typo like 'invarients' must fail loudly, not pass silently); adopter-specific keys belong under the explicit `extensions` namespace.

purpose.description: What the system is for (PROFILE.md section 6.1).

non_goals.description: Explicit non-goals — what the system deliberately does not do (PROFILE.md section 6.1 requires non-goals).

system.description: As-built system description identifying the system, its principal responsibilities and material boundaries, and known material limitations or unknowns, as required by PROFILE.md section 6.1. When absent, keep a separate system file mapped via adoption.yaml `paths.system` (default `assurance/SYSTEM.md`); the validator requires one of the two.

invariants.description: Invariant register section (required at core — at least one entry; PROFILE.md section 6.1). Items follow the entry shape of invariants.schema.json; the validator validates this section against that schema — the shape is not duplicated here.

defeaters.description: Defeater register section. Items follow the entry shape of defeaters.schema.json; the validator validates this section against that schema — the shape is not duplicated here.

residuals.description: Residual register section (at least one entry). Items follow the entry shape of residuals.schema.json; the validator validates this section against that schema — the shape is not duplicated here.

extensions.description: Free-form local extension namespace — the only place for adopter-specific keys. Top-level unknown keys are rejected so that typos in section names fail loudly.
